summary: This Playwright system shows low trust due to systemic locator instability, weak diagnosability, and shared-state risk. The current signal is not strong enough for safe acceleration.

topFindings.title: Brittle locator strategy
topFindings.reason: Selectors are fragile and likely to cause recurring false failures.

topFindings.title: Rerun-dependent debugging
topFindings.reason: Evidence quality is not sufficient for first-pass diagnosis.

topFindings.title: Shared mutable state
topFindings.reason: Execution order and state leakage reduce result trustworthiness.
